2026-04-16T03:06:17.1217007Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1217844Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1218834Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1219710Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1220206Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1220583Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1220835Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1221123Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1221394Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1221754Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1222066Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1222510Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-16T03:06:17.1222935Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-16T03:10:07.7126893Z **Summary of gaps found in `Azure.Communication.Messages` (triggered by PR #56911):**
2026-04-16T03:10:07.7132882Z - `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, and new `ConversationThreadClientSettings` are completely absent from the README
2026-04-16T03:10:07.7139028Z - The **Key concepts** section only lists `NotificationMessagesClient`
2026-04-16T03:10:08.9939245Z - No README examples exist for any Conversation API operation
2026-04-16T03:10:08.9959153Z - Auto-generated sample files exist in `tests/Samples/` but have no `#region Snippet:` regions wired to README placeholders
2026-04-16T03:10:09.5472727Z - `CHANGELOG 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` is empty despite the migration adding new public types

2026-04-16T03:10:13.5909381Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T03:07:44.038290594Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n## 1.3.0-beta.2 (2025-10-01)\n\n### Features Added\n- Add support for read receipt and typing indicator.\n\n## 1.3.0-beta.1 (2025-04-14)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Add Unified Messaging.\n\n## 1.2.0-beta.1 (2025-02-11)\n\n### Features Added\n- Added Interactive Message.\n- Added Reaction Message.\n- Added Sticker Message.\n- Exposed `JsonModelWriteCore` for model serialization procedure.\n\n## 1.1.0 (2024-10-18)\n\n### Features Added\n- Added support for additional media message types: documents, videos, and audio.\n- Introduced the following new models for enhanced media notifications:\n  - `DocumentNotificationContent`: for sending document notifications.\n  - `VideoNotificationContent`: for sending video notifications.\n  - `AudioNotificationContent`: for sending audio notifications.\n  - `ImageNotificationContent`: for sending image notifications.\n\n### Other Changes\n- The `MediaNotificationContent` model, previously used for sending image notifications, is being deprecated. A deprecation warning will inform users to transition to the new `ImageNotificationContent` model for future use.\n\n## 1.0.0 (2024-02-29)\n\n### Features Added\n- Introduced additional constructors for `MessageTemplateClient` and `NotificationMessagesClient` to support `TokenCredential` authentication methods.\n- Introduced a variety of Notification Content models such as `MediaNotificationContent`, `TemplateNotificationContent`, and `TextNotificationContent`, enabling more polymorphic notification types.\n- Introduced a variety of message template value models such as `MessageTemplateDocument`, `MessageTemplateImage`, `MessageTemplateVideo`, etc., enabling more polymorphic message template values.\n- Added new namespace specific to WhatsApp message templates, including `WhatsAppMessageTemplateBindings`, `WhatsAppMessageTemplateBindingsButton`, and `WhatsAppMessageButtonSubType`, etc.\n- Updated `CommunicationMessagesClientOptions` to include a new service version `V2024_02_01` and expanded `CommunicationMessagesModelFactory` with factory methods for new model types.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n- Removed the `SendMessageOptions` class in favor of more granular method overloads (`NotificationContent` subclasses) in `NotificationMessagesClient`.\n- Changed the way to construct MessageTemplate including message template bindings and values.\n- Changed `MessageTemplateItem` class  to support polymorphism, with the introduction of a child class `WhatsAppMessageTemplateItem` containing WhatsApp-specific template contracts.\n- Added `GeoPosition`` struct to encapsulate geographic coordinates into a single entity. This change affects any functionality that previously required separate latitude and longitude inputs. \n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (2023-08-15)\n\nThis is the first Public Preview release of Azure Communication Services for advanced messages. For more information, please see the [README][read_me].\n\nThis is a Public Preview version, so breaking changes are possible in subsequent releases as we improve the product. To provide feedback, please submit an issue in our [Azure SDK for .NET GitHub repo][issues].\n\n\u003c!-- LINKS --\u003e\n[read_me]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md\n[issues]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-16T03:10:13.5933280Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T03:07:44.046819237Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure Communication Messages client library for .NET\n\nThis package contains a C# SDK for Azure Communication Messages Services.\n\n[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][package] | [Product documentation][product_docs]\n\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\nInstall the Azure Communication Messages client library for .NET with [NuGet][nuget]:\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Communication.Messages\n``` \n\n### Prerequisites\nYou need an [Azure subscription][azure_sub] and a [Communication Service Resource][communication_resource_docs] to use this package.\n\nTo create a new Communication Service, you can use the [Azure Portal][communication_resource_create_portal], the [Azure PowerShell][communication_resource_create_power_shell], or the [.NET management client library][communication_resource_create_net].\n\nYou must register your WhatsApp business account and have an active channel before sending messages. See [Quickstart: Register WhatsApp business account][azuredocs_register_waba] for more details\n\n### Key concepts\n`NotificationMessagesClient` provides the functionality to send notification messages.\n\n### Using statements\n```C#\nusing Azure.Communication.Messages;\n```\n\n### Authenticate the client\n#### Connection string\n\nThe NotificationMessagesClient is used for sending messages.\nThe MessageTemplateClient is used for retrieving template information.\n\nFor simplicity, this example uses a connection string to authenticate. In production environments, we recommend using Microsoft Entra ID.\n\nMessages clients can be authenticated using the connection string acquired from an Azure Communication Resource in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].\n\n```C#\nvar connectionString = \"\u003cConnectionString\u003e\"; // Find your Communication Services resource in the Azure portal\nNotificationMessagesClient notificationMessagesClient = new NotificationMessagesClient(connectionString);\nMessageTemplateClient messageTemplateClient = new MessageTemplateClient(connectionString);\n```\n\n## Examples\n### Send a notification message\nTo send a notification message, call the `Send` or `SendAsync` function from the `NotificationMessagesClient`.\n\n#### Send a text message\n```C#\n// Create the recipient list, currently only one recipient is supported \nvar recipientList = new List\u003cstring\u003e { \"\u003cRecipientPhoneNumber\u003e\" };\nvar textContent = new TextNotificationContent(new Guid(\"\u003cchannel-registration-id\u003e\"), recipientList, \"Come on everyone, let's go for lunch together.\");\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(textContent);\nConsole.WriteLine($\"Message id: {result.Receipts[0].MessageId}\");\n```\n\n#### Send a template message\n```C#\n// Create the recipient list, currently only one recipient is supported \nvar recipientList = new List\u003cstring\u003e { \"\u003cRecipientPhoneNumber\u003e\" };\nstring templateName = \"sample_template\";\nstring templateLanguage = \"en_us\";\nvar messageTemplate = new MessageTemplate(templateName, templateLanguage);\nvar templateContent = new TemplateNotificationContent(channelRegistrationId, recipientList, messageTemplate);\nSendMessageResult result = await notificationMessagesClient.SendAsync(temp
